reforms.description: The Government of Israel aims to capitalize on its strong artificial intelligence (AI) industry by maintaining a flexible regulatory approach and enhancing collaboration between higher-education institutions and AI firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between academia and industry is expected to drive innovation and productivity in the AI sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative was outlined as part of the government's strategy to strengthen the AI sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the AI ecosystem, which is crucial for maintaining Israel's competitive edge in technology.
reforms.source_quote: Capitalising on an already strong artificial intelligence (AI) industry is essential, by maintaining a flexible regulatory approach and further nurturing links between higher-education institutions and AI firms.

reforms.description: The authorities are investing to expand computing capacity available to research universities and early-stage startups, aiming to strengthen the foundation for AI development in Israel.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing computing capacity will facilitate advanced research and innovation in AI, contributing to productivity gains in the high-tech sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities announced plans to invest in expanding computing capacity for research universities and startups.
reforms.importance_rationale: This investment is significant for bolstering the research infrastructure necessary for AI development, which is crucial for maintaining Israel's competitive edge in the high-tech sector.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities are investing to expand computing capacity available to research universities and early-stage startups.

reforms.description: Public authorities stepped in with emergency help for startups, which evolved into more permanent support programmes aimed at fostering innovation in the high-tech sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing support to startups enhances their survival and growth prospects, which is vital for innovation and economic recovery.
reforms.status_evidence: Emergency help for startups has evolved into permanent support programmes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The transition from emergency support to permanent programmes indicates a significant commitment to fostering a resilient startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: In late 2023, public authorities stepped in with emergency help for startups, which then evolved into more permanent support programmes.

reforms.description: Israel is enhancing its investment in scientific research and higher education to strengthen its position in the global AI market, addressing the gender gap and promoting participation from under-represented groups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in education and research will enhance the skill set of the workforce, fostering innovation and productivity in the AI sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative to invest in scientific research and education has been publicly announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance human capital in a critical sector, with potential long-term benefits for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Further investment in scientific research and higher education would reinforce the sector.

reforms.description: The authorities are preparing to build an AI High-Performance Computing laboratory as part of the National AI Programme 2024-2027, aimed at expanding local capacity for academic institutions, startups, and emerging AI companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing computing infrastructure will facilitate advanced research and development in AI, thereby boosting productivity and innovation in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities are preparing to build an AI High-Performance Computing laboratory.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in research infrastructure that is crucial for the development of AI capabilities in Israel, impacting both academic and commercial sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities are preparing to build an AI High-Performance Computing laboratory as part of the NIS 1 billion (USD 270 million) National AI Programme 2024-2027.

reforms.description: The Israel Innovation Authority and the Ministry of Finance launched 'fast-track' grants aimed at early-stage high-tech startups, including those in AI, to support their survival following the 7 October 2023 attack.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing immediate financial support to startups helps maintain innovation momentum and prevents the loss of valuable entrepreneurial talent in the high-tech sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was aimed at early-stage high-tech startups with a survival horizon below six months.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is a significant response to an urgent need for financial support in the startup ecosystem, helping to stabilize the sector during a crisis.
reforms.source_quote: The Israel Innovation Authority and the Ministry of Finance launched 'fast-track' grants at the end of October 2023.

reforms.description: The National Artificial Intelligence Programme aims to secure Israel's position as a global leader in AI innovation and technology, focusing on investments in AI infrastructure and promoting AI adoption across the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering AI research and infrastructure, the programme is expected to enhance productivity and innovation across various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was launched through Government Resolution No. 173 in February 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a comprehensive strategy to enhance Israel's AI capabilities, involving multiple sectors and significant investment, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The National AI Programme aims to secure Israel’s position as a global leader in AI innovation and technology.

reforms.description: The Israeli government has launched calls for proposals for data-based applied AI research in collaboration between academia and public bodies, allocating funds to researchers and coordinating access to public sector datasets.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates collaboration between academia and public bodies, enhancing research capabilities and promoting the application of AI technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has allocated funds and coordinated access to datasets for researchers.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance AI research capabilities and collaboration, which is crucial for advancing Israel's position in AI technology.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Innovation, Science and Technology in collaboration with the National Digital Agency has launched calls for proposals for data-based applied AI research.

reforms.description: The Ministry of Innovation launched a competitive mechanism for national projects to incorporate AI in the public sector, providing budgetary and professional support for selected initiatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative enhances the efficiency of public services through AI, potentially leading to cost savings and improved service delivery.
reforms.status_evidence: The ministry approved nine selected projects initiated in March 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step in integrating AI into public services, with the potential for broad impacts across various government functions.
reforms.source_quote: The ministry of innovation launched a competitive mechanism for national projects to incorporate AI in the public sector.

reforms.description: The Israel Innovation Authority is supporting high-tech startups, particularly in AI, through direct investment and enhancing the yield of institutional investors providing venture capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating investment in AI startups, this reform aims to boost innovation and economic growth in the high-tech sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The authority is actively supporting high-tech startups through direct investments.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for enhancing the startup ecosystem, particularly in the rapidly evolving AI sector.
reforms.source_quote: The Israel Innovation Authority is supporting high-tech startups (half of which are AI) through direct investment.

reforms.description: The government approved a gradual hike in the rate of the carbon tax applied on fossil fuels from 2025 to 2030, which aims to reflect environmental costs and encourage emission reductions across all sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The carbon tax is expected to create economic incentives for reducing emissions, promoting cleaner technologies and energy efficiency, which can enhance productivity and innovation in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax is in force since 1 January 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in Israel's approach to carbon emissions, aiming to align with international standards and support the transition to a low-carbon economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government in January 2024 approved a gradual hike in the rate of the tax applied on each type of fossil fuel from 2025-2030.

reforms.description: Israel has adopted new green building standards that require all new mid and large-sized constructions to exceed minimum levels of insulation and performance of appliances, effective from September 2023.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing energy efficiency in buildings reduces energy consumption and promotes sustainable construction practices, contributing to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new regulation mandating energy efficiency standards for new constructions is now in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in building regulations, aiming to improve energy efficiency across a large segment of new constructions, which is crucial for Israel's long-term sustainability goals.
reforms.source_quote: Since September 2023, all new mid and large-sized constructions must exceed minimum levels of insulation and performance of appliances under Standard 5281.

reforms.description: The Israeli government approved the 'What is good for Europe is good for Israel' reform, which aligns Israeli regulatory requirements with EU standards for approximately 90% of consumer products, foods, and cosmetics. This initiative allows products deemed safe in the EU to enter Israel without separate testing, significantly reducing bureaucratic hurdles and facilitating imports.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing regulatory barriers and aligning with EU standards, the reform enhances competition and lowers import prices, which can stimulate economic activity and consumer welfare.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was approved by the Knesset and is set to take effect in January 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in trade policy, enhancing Israel's integration with the EU market and potentially leading to substantial economic benefits.
reforms.source_quote: The 'What is good for Europe is good for Israel' reform approved by the Knesset in July 2024 and in force since January 2025 seeks to align Israeli regulatory requirements with those of the EU for multiple product categories.

reforms.description: In 2023, the Planning Authority released new guidelines to facilitate the transition between detailed local plans and the licensing process, aiming to improve licensing procedures and shorten approval timelines.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining the licensing process is expected to enhance construction efficiency, thereby increasing housing supply and reducing costs.
reforms.status_evidence: The Planning Authority has released new guidelines aimed at improving the licensing process.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant bottleneck in the housing supply chain, which is crucial for meeting demand in a densely populated area.
reforms.source_quote: The Planning Authority released a set of new guidelines aimed at facilitating the transition between detailed local plans and the licensing process.

reforms.description: The 'plan plus permit' reform implemented in 2024 streamlines the approval process by allowing urban renewal plans and permits to be granted in a single hearing, potentially reducing approval timelines by one to four years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By expediting the approval process, this reform is expected to enhance the efficiency of urban renewal projects, thereby increasing housing supply.
reforms.status_evidence: The 'plan plus permit' reform was implemented to streamline the approval process.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly improves the efficiency of urban renewal processes, which is vital for addressing housing shortages in urban areas.
reforms.source_quote: The 'plan plus permit' reform was implemented in 2024 to streamline the approval process.
